reasoning: The base rate for a financial spread moving higher in the very short term (e.g., 1 week) can be modeled using the FRED statistical trend tool, which outputs an estimate of 41% due to recent mean reversion from a local peak of 1.20 down to 1.18. For longer horizons (1 month and 3 months), the series behaves more like a random walk with slight upward drift, increasing uncertainty. Hence, the probabilities revert toward 0.5: 46% for the 1-month horizon (January 20, 2026), and 49% for the 3-month horizon (March 21, 2026).
